title: Errors, Cleanup, and Root-Cause Debugging · State Forensics
summary: Diagnose malformed records and failed operations while keeping cleanup, user recovery, and causal evidence intact. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a recoverable import and diagnostics workflow and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Differentiate syntax, reference, type, range, aggregate, DOM, and domain errors by evidence and recovery boundary.
Throw Error objects with stable messages, structured context, and preserved causes without exposing secrets.
Place try, catch, and finally around the smallest recoverable boundary without swallowing failures or overriding control flow.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Resource Cleanup evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Resource Cleanup assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Import 6 rows containing one malformed record and one simulated storage failure. Preserve the original error cause, clean up resources, recover only the expected row failure, and let programming defects surface. Competency probe: Release listeners, timers, streams, locks, and disposable resources on success, failure, and cancellation.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Resource Cleanup has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Resource Cleanup precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Import 6 rows containing one malformed record and one simulated storage failure. Preserve the original error cause, clean up resources, recover only the expected row failure, and let programming defects surface. Competency probe: Release listeners, timers, streams, locks, and disposable resources on success, failure, and cancellation.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Garbage collection automatically cancels all external resources.
